context.story_engine: Noble houses compete for power across multiple kingdoms, testing how far they'll go for survival and control

plotlines.name: Ned: Honor
plotlines.goal: uncover the truth about Jon Arryn's death and protect the realm
plotlines.obstacle: court intrigue, Lannister power, his own moral inflexibility
plotlines.stakes: his life, his family's safety, the stability of the realm

plotlines.name: Cersei: Power
plotlines.goal: protect her children's claim to the throne and maintain Lannister control
plotlines.obstacle: Ned's investigation, Robert's potential discovery of the truth
plotlines.stakes: her children's lives, her own execution

plotlines.name: Jon: Belonging
plotlines.goal: find his place and purpose at the Night's Watch
plotlines.obstacle: class differences with recruits, discovering the Watch's reality

plotlines.name: Daenerys: Transformation
plotlines.goal: evolve from powerless exile to true leader
plotlines.obstacle: Dothraki culture, Viserys's control, her own inexperience
plotlines.stakes: remaining a victim, losing her identity

plotlines.name: Tyrion: Survival
plotlines.goal: navigate accusations and family conflicts through wit
plotlines.obstacle: false accusations, hostile captors, family expectations

plotlines.name: Catelyn: Protection
plotlines.goal: protect her family from Lannister threats
plotlines.obstacle: limited information, political complexity, distance from family

plotlines.name: Robb: War
plotlines.goal: rescue his father and defend the North
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister military power, political alliances, inexperience

plotlines.name: Viserys: Crown
plotlines.goal: reclaim the Iron Throne using Dothraki forces
plotlines.obstacle: his own arrogance, Dothraki customs, Daenerys's growing power

events.event: Three Night's Watch rangers discover wildlings slaughtered in ritual pattern by White Walkers
White Walker kills two rangers, Will flees south
Ned executes deserter Will for abandoning his post
Stark children find dead direwolf with pups, each claims one
Jon claims the albino runt direwolf and names it Ghost
Raven brings news of Jon Arryn's death and Robert's approach
King Robert arrives at Winterfell with the Lannisters
Robert visits Lyanna's crypt and asks Ned to be Hand of the King
Robert proposes marriage between Joffrey and Sansa
Viserys arranges Daenerys's marriage to Khal Drogo for his army
Daenerys submits to the marriage arrangement despite her terror
Jorah Mormont presents himself to Daenerys at the wedding
Catelyn receives Lysa's letter accusing Lannisters of murdering Jon Arryn
Ned decides he must go south to investigate Jon Arryn's death
Bran discovers Cersei and Jaime in sexual embrace while climbing
Jaime pushes Bran from the tower window to protect their secret

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore how dangerous knowledge threatens those who possess it
Bran's discovery connects Ned's investigation, Cersei's secret, and Catelyn's fears
the audience knows the Lannisters' secret that Ned is unknowingly investigating

patches.reason: White Walker events don't fit existing plotlines - need supernatural/existential threat line

events.event: Maester Luwin tells Catelyn and Robb that Bran will never walk again if he lives
Catelyn refuses to leave Bran's bedside, neglecting Winterfell's affairs
Jaime expresses unease to Cersei about what Bran might remember
Cersei dismisses the risk, confident a child's word means nothing against theirs
Jon decides to join the Night's Watch and travels north with Tyrion
Jon gives Arya a small sword she names Needle
Jon says farewell to the unconscious Bran
Ned departs Winterfell for King's Landing with Sansa and Arya
Sansa and Joffrey encounter Arya sparring with Mycah by the Trident
Joffrey threatens Mycah with his sword
Nymeria bites Joffrey's arm in defense of Mycah
Arya drives Nymeria away to save the wolf from punishment
Robert holds court on the matter and Cersei demands a direwolf be killed
Robert orders Sansa's innocent direwolf Lady executed instead
Ned carries out the sentence himself, executing Lady
The Hound hunts down and kills the butcher's boy Mycah
Daenerys struggles with painful nights with Drogo and grueling days of riding
Jorah rides alongside Daenerys and explains Dothraki customs
Daenerys learns from her handmaiden how to assert herself with Drogo physically
Daenerys faces Drogo as an equal rather than a victim, shifting their marriage dynamic
An assassin enters Bran's room with a Valyrian steel dagger to kill him
Catelyn fights the assassin bare-handed until Bran's direwolf kills the man
Catelyn resolves to ride south secretly to warn Ned about the Lannister threat

episodes.theme: the cost of honor in a corrupt world

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters forced to compromise their principles or identity to survive
the audience knows the Lannisters are behind Bran's fall and the assassination attempt, but Catelyn only suspects
Ned's journey south brings him into direct conflict with Cersei's need to protect her secrets

events.event: Jon Snow arrives at Castle Black and discovers most recruits are criminals and outcasts
Jon's superior combat training makes him dominant in practice, earning resentment from other recruits
Tyrion advises Jon to stop acting like a privileged lord's son and help the weaker boys
Jon begins helping the weaker boys train
Ned arrives in King's Landing and takes his seat on the Small Council
Ned meets Littlefinger, Varys, and Grand Maester Pycelle on the Small Council
Ned learns the Crown owes millions to the Lannisters and Iron Bank due to Robert's spending
Robert demands a lavish tournament in Ned's honor despite Ned's objections
Catelyn arrives in King's Landing in secret
Littlefinger arranges a private meeting between Catelyn and Ned
Catelyn shows Ned the Valyrian steel dagger used in Bran's assassination attempt
Littlefinger identifies the dagger as one he lost to Tyrion Lannister in a bet
Ned and Catelyn agree she must return north while he investigates from court
Arya chases cats through the Red Keep and stumbles into the cellar
Arya overhears two shadowy men discussing Stark-Lannister tension and delaying war
Arya fails to convince Ned of the significance of what she overheard
Daenerys commands the khalasar to halt so she can explore on foot
Viserys strikes Daenerys for defying his authority
Daenerys's Dothraki guards intervene and she allows them to humiliate Viserys
Viserys realizes his sister is no longer under his control and the Dothraki respect her

episodes.theme: the cost of privilege and the necessity of earning respect

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters learning they must earn respect rather than expect it based on birth or position
Ned and Catelyn's investigation merges when she brings him evidence about the dagger
the audience knows the shadowy conspirators Arya overhears are likely plotting against the Starks, but Ned dismisses her warning

events.event: Ned visits the blacksmith's apprentice Gendry, discovering he has black hair and resembles young Robert
Ned realizes Robert has bastard children throughout King's Landing, all with black hair
Ned obtains the genealogical history book Jon Arryn was studying before his death
Tyrion arrives at Winterfell and gives Bran designs for a special saddle to help him ride
Robb receives Tyrion coldly, suspicious of all Lannisters
Daenerys discovers she is pregnant with Drogo's child
Jorah tells Daenerys privately that a Targaryen heir changes the political calculation in Westeros
Daenerys announces her pregnancy to Drogo, who declares the child will be the Stallion Who Mounts the World
Viserys grows increasingly bitter that the Dothraki show him no deference
Ser Gregor Clegane kills Ser Hugh of the Vale during the tournament joust
Littlefinger tells Sansa the story of how Gregor burned the Hound's face as a child
Ned suspects Ser Hugh's death was not accidental and that witnesses are being eliminated
Catelyn recognizes Tyrion among travelers at the Crossroads Inn
Catelyn calls on her father's bannermen and arrests Tyrion for Bran's attempted murder
Catelyn takes Tyrion east toward the Eyrie to avoid Lannister pursuit

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters acting on dangerous knowledge or suspicions that could change everything
Catelyn's arrest of Tyrion directly threatens his survival while serving her protective goals
Ned's investigation threatens Cersei's power, but she doesn't yet know how close he is to the truth

patches.reason: Littlefinger's storytelling to Sansa serves broader court manipulation but doesn't fit existing plotlines

events.event: Robert receives word that Daenerys is pregnant and orders her assassination
Ned argues fiercely against murdering Daenerys and her unborn child
Robert refuses to relent on the assassination order
Ned resigns as Hand of the King in protest
Varys warns Ned that his investigation has made him a target and Jon Arryn was poisoned
Catelyn's party is ambushed by hill tribes on the road to the Eyrie
Tyrion fights alongside his captors to survive the ambush
Catelyn's party reaches the Eyrie with Tyrion as prisoner
Lysa Arryn imprisons Tyrion in the sky cells
Jon Snow is assigned to the stewards rather than the rangers
Sam explains that the Lord Commander's steward is being groomed for command
Arya practices sword fighting with Syrio Forel
Ned watches Arya's lesson and realizes war is coming for his family
Littlefinger takes Ned to a brothel to see another of Robert's bastards
Ned realizes all of Cersei's children are golden-haired while Robert's bastards have black hair
Ned discovers that Joffrey, Myrcella, and Tommen are products of Cersei and Jaime's incest
Jaime confronts Ned in the streets with armed men over Tyrion's arrest
Jaime's soldiers kill Ned's guards and Jaime wounds Ned before riding away

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters choosing between pragmatic compromise and moral/personal principles
Ned's discovery of the incest secret directly threatens Cersei's power, leading to Jaime's violent response
the audience knows Tyrion's arrest will provoke Lannister retaliation while Catelyn believes she's protecting her family

events.event: Ned wakes from his injuries to find Robert has returned from hunting
Robert demands Ned resume his duties as Hand, threatening to give the position to Jaime if he refuses
Ned reluctantly agrees to continue as Hand
Robert rides out hunting again, leaving Ned to sit the Iron Throne
Villagers petition the court reporting Ser Gregor Clegane's raids in the Riverlands
Ned recognizes this as Lannister provocation to draw the Tullys into conflict
Ned sends Lord Beric Dondarrion to bring Gregor to justice
Ned summons Tywin Lannister to answer for his bannerman's crimes
Tyrion demands a trial by combat rather than face Lysa's biased judgment
Tyrion recruits the sellsword Bronn to fight as his champion
Bronn defeats Ser Vardis Egen using speed and cunning against armored strength
Lysa is forced to release Tyrion, who walks out free with Bronn
Viserys arrives drunk at a Dothraki feast and draws his sword in Vaes Dothrak
Viserys threatens Daenerys and demands the crown Drogo promised him
Drogo melts gold and pours it over Viserys's head, killing him
Daenerys watches her brother die without flinching and declares he was no dragon
Jorah observes Daenerys's transformation with admiration and concern
Jon Snow and Sam take their vows before a heart tree beyond the Wall
Jon's direwolf Ghost emerges carrying a severed human hand
Ned studies the genealogy book and confirms all Baratheons produce black-haired children
Ned holds the secret that could destroy the Lannisters but wrestles with how to use it

episodes.theme: the price of truth and honor

interactions.description: all plotlines explore characters facing defining moments that reveal their true nature
the audience knows Ned has discovered the truth about Joffrey's parentage while the Lannisters remain unaware
Ned's actions as Hand directly precipitate the coming military conflict with the Lannisters

events.event: Ned confronts Cersei in the godswood, revealing he knows her children are Jaime's
Ned offers Cersei the chance to flee with her children before Robert returns
Cersei tells Ned that in the game of thrones, you win or you die
Robert is brought back mortally wounded by a boar during the hunt
Robert dictates his will to Ned, naming him Protector of the Realm
Ned writes 'rightful heir' instead of Joffrey's name in Robert's will
Robert dies
Ned sends a letter to Stannis informing him of the truth about Cersei's children
Ned asks Littlefinger to secure the City Watch's loyalty
Littlefinger advises Ned to make peace with the Lannisters and rule through Joffrey
Ned refuses to compromise with what he sees as a usurper
Renly offers Ned backing of his household guard to seize Joffrey
Ned refuses Renly's offer, unwilling to frighten a child with soldiers
Renly flees King's Landing
A wine merchant tries to poison Daenerys on Robert's orders
Jorah Mormont intervenes and exposes the assassination plot
The assassination attempt enrages Drogo, who vows to conquer Westeros
Two dead rangers' bodies are brought inside Castle Black
The corpses reanimate as wights in the night
Jon fights a wight attacking the Lord Commander and saves him by setting it on fire

episodes.theme: the price of honor in a world without it

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore the cost of moral choices—Ned's mercy, Cersei's ruthlessness, and Daenerys's awakening to power
the audience knows Robert's assassination attempt on Daenerys while watching his deathbed scene with Ned
Ned and Cersei's confrontation directly connects their opposing goals and sets up the final conflict

events.event: Ned enters the throne room to present Robert's will naming him Protector of the Realm
Cersei tears up the will and declares Joffrey king
On Littlefinger's signal, the gold cloaks turn on Ned's men, killing his household guard
Littlefinger puts a dagger to Ned's throat, telling him he warned not to trust him
Ned is arrested and imprisoned in the dungeons beneath the Red Keep
Sansa is kept in her chambers under Lannister guard
Syrio Forel fights the Lannister soldiers with a wooden practice sword, buying Arya time to flee
Syrio is overwhelmed and presumably killed
Arya recovers Needle and hides in the streets of King's Landing
Drogo's khalasar raids a village to fund the invasion of Westeros
Daenerys orders the Dothraki to stop raping the captive women, taking them under her protection
Mago challenges Daenerys's order, angering Drogo's warriors
Drogo fights Mago in single combat, killing him bare-handed despite taking a chest wound
Mirri Maz Duur offers to treat Drogo's wound, and Daenerys accepts her help
Lord Commander Mormont rewards Jon with Longclaw for saving his life from the wight
Jon receives word that his father has been arrested for treason and attempts to desert
Sam, Grenn, and Pyp convince Jon to honor his vow and stay at the Wall
Tyrion recruits the hill tribes by promising Tywin's gold and weapons
Tyrion arrives at Tywin's war camp where an army is assembling against the Riverlands

episodes.theme: the cost of honor in a world without it

interactions.description: all three plotlines explore characters choosing between personal desires and higher duties
Ned's investigation and Cersei's protection of her children collide in the throne room confrontation
Jon doesn't know the full extent of his father's peril when deciding whether to desert

events.event: Robb Stark calls the northern bannermen to war and marches south with twenty thousand men to rescue his father
Catelyn negotiates passage at the Twins by promising Robb will marry one of Frey's daughters and Arya will marry one of Frey's sons
Robb splits his forces, sending a feint toward Tywin's army while leading the bulk to Riverrun
The feint engages Tywin's forces in the Battle of the Green Fork, where Tyrion fights and is knocked unconscious
Tywin wins the battle but realizes too late that the main Stark army was not there
Robb's force ambushes Jaime's army at the Battle of the Whispering Wood and wins decisively
Robb personally captures Jaime Lannister
Catelyn confronts the captive Jaime, who taunts her but cannot deny his vulnerability
Varys visits Ned in the dungeon and urges him to confess to treason for Sansa's sake
Ned agrees to lie for his daughter's sake, abandoning his principles
Ned publicly confesses that he conspired against the rightful king
Joffrey orders Ned's execution despite Cersei and the councilors expecting mercy
Ser Ilyn Payne beheads Ned Stark with his own sword as Sansa screams
Arya is grabbed by Yoren who shields her eyes and pulls her away
Drogo's wound festers into a deadly infection
Daenerys desperately asks Mirri Maz Duur to use blood magic to save Drogo
Daenerys goes into premature labor and is carried into the tent during the dark magic ritual

episodes.theme: the price of power and honor

interactions.description: all plotlines explore the cost of choices—Ned's honor costs his life, Joffrey's power corrupts mercy, Robb's war demands sacrifice, Daenerys's desperation risks everything
Ned's execution is the climax of both his honor arc and Cersei's power struggle, with Joffrey's decision affecting both
Robb wins his battles while his father dies—the audience knows the victory is hollow

events.event: Daenerys wakes to find Drogo alive but in a vegetative state, his mind destroyed by blood magic
Daenerys discovers her son was stillborn and monstrous, the price for Drogo's life
Mirri Maz Duur reveals she acted deliberately as revenge, telling Daenerys she saved no one
Most of the khalasar abandons Daenerys for a new khal, leaving her with few followers
Jorah urges Daenerys to flee east, but she chooses a different path
Joffrey forces Sansa to look at Ned's head mounted on a spike
Sansa nearly pushes Joffrey from the rampart but the Hound stops her
The Hound quietly wipes blood from Sansa's lip after Joffrey has a guard strike her
Arya travels north disguised as a boy with Yoren and Night's Watch recruits
Yoren has cut Arya's hair and renamed her Arry to hide her identity
Gendry travels with the recruits, unknowingly fleeing Lannisters hunting Robert's bastards
Robb's bannermen debate their next move at the Twins
Robb proposes marching south to avenge Ned but refuses to serve a Baratheon king
Northern lords reject all Baratheon claimants and Greatjon Umber declares Robb King in the North
The northern lords kneel and chant for Robb as King in the North
Catelyn visits captive Jaime and strikes him for what the Lannisters have done
Lord Commander Mormont tells Jon his duty lies with the Watch, not the war of kings
Mormont announces a great ranging to find Benjen and investigate the White Walkers
Jon rides out with the Night's Watch into the haunted forest
Daenerys builds a funeral pyre for Drogo and places her dragon eggs on it
Daenerys ties Mirri Maz Duur to the flames as a final sacrifice
Daenerys walks into the fire herself over Jorah's protests
Daenerys emerges unburned from the ashes with three living dragons
The remaining Dothraki kneel before Daenerys and her dragons

interactions.description: all three plotlines involve characters choosing new identities and purposes from the ashes of their old lives
the audience knows Ned is dead while watching the consequences ripple through both King's Landing and the North
Ned's execution creates the catalyst that drives all other plotlines in this episode

patches.reason: Arya's escape and disguise represents a distinct survival plotline separate from existing threads
Viserys plotline has no events in this episode - may have concluded in previous episode
Tyrion plotline has no events in this episode - unclear if still active
